# TomoTwin

Particle picking in Tomograms using triplet networks and metric learning

![TomoTwin Logo](resources/images/TomoTwin_black_transparent_cropped.png#gh-light-mode-only)
![TomoTwin Logo](resources/images/TomoTwin_white_transparent_cropped.png#gh-dark-mode-only)

## Instructions

For installation instructions and tutorials please see our [documentation](https://tomotwin-cryoet.readthedocs.io/)

Represents the configuration for training

Path to folder with PDB volumes

All output files are written here.

param max_neg

Maximum number of triplets generated for a anchor-positive pair.

Configuration file for the network.

Specific Implementation of the TripletProvider base class. Uses simple filename matching to create the FilePathTriplets.
It is no using any PDB files. Instead it will generate all possible anchor-positive pairs and then select the negative randomly.

classes.name: tomotwin.modules.networks.GeneralizedMeanPooling.GeneralizedMeanPooling
documentation:
Applies a 3D power-average adaptive pooling over an input signal composed of several input planes.
The function computed is: $f(X) = pow(sum(pow(X, p)), 1/p)$

> - At p = infinity, one gets Max Pooling
> - At p = 1, one gets Average Pooling

The output is of size H x W x D, for any input size.
The number of output features is equal to the number of input planes.
Args:

> output_size: the target output size of the image of the form H x W x D.
>
> : Can be a tuple (H, W, D) or a single H for a square image H x H x H
>   H and W can be either a `int`, or `None` which means the size will
>   be the same as that of the input.
